text.title: requirements-lifecycle Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for requirements-lifecycle as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No version control — teams work from the "latest" document without knowing if they have the current version.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use numbered versions and a single authoritative location; version history must be auditable to support compliance reviews.

failure-mode.description: Skipping validation — assuming requirements are correct because stakeholders provided them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stakeholders state what they think they want, not necessarily what they need; walkthroughs and prototype reviews surface misunderstandings before development starts.

failure-mode.description: Status not updated — requirements tracking becomes fiction because nobody updates state after approvals and implementations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Define who is responsible for status updates at each stage transition and make it a team ritual, not an optional task.

failure-mode.description: No baseline — cannot compare current requirements to approved version to measure scope creep.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Baseline at each major milestone (project kickoff, sprint planning, release) and store the baseline snapshot separately from the working document.
